title: carduus undulatus var. douglasii
intro: Carduus undulatus var. douglasii, also known as Douglas' bitter thistle, is a variety of thistle plant that is native to the western United States and Canada. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include acting as a bitter digestive aid and a stimulant to the digestive system, as well as having anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which are responsible for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Douglas' bitter thistle include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
